arts.ttl: Former Reading midfielder 'training' with Derby County ahead of Championship return
arts.sum: Former Reading midfielder Jeff Hendrick is training with Championship side Derby County, manager John Eustace has confirmed. The 33-year-old has not played for 12 months since leaving Newcastle United and spent the 2022/23 campaign with the Royals, making nearly 50 appearances as the club were relegated to League One. Aside from a 12 match spell with Sheffield Wednesday last season, Hendrick has not played week-in and week-out since departing the SCL Stadium.

arts.ttl: Cyriel Dessers reading same Rangers script as Jose Mourinho as Ibrox urged to drown out comeback chat
arts.sum: The big striker helped fillet Fenerbahce in Istanbul but warns Turks will be team on a mission in Glasgow for return leg

arts.ttl: Berkshire MPs speak out in Parliament during Reading and Football Governance debate
arts.sum: Every Member of Parliament for constituencies in Berkshire spoke up in favour of the Football Governance Bill in Westminster Hall on Thursday. MP for Woodley and Earley, Yuan Yang, called for more powers for the Government’s Independent Football Regulator, the legislation for which has been stalled by Conservative peers in the House of Lords. Prior to the debate, Ms. Yang launched a petition with fan groups Sell Before We Dai and the Supporters Trust at Reading calling for a Parliamentary inquiry to hold Reading’s absent owners to account. The petition reached 10,000 signatures in just a week.

Crawley were heading for a crucial victory at Lincoln on Tuesday after going ahead, but a collapse from then on in saw them lose again and slip seven points from safety. The Red Devils have only won ...

arts.ttl: Exeter City boss praises 'outstanding' fourth official in Reading stalemate
arts.sum: Exeter City boss Gary Caldwell praised fourth official David Rock for his performance in Tuesday's 0-0 draw with Reading, but was left less enamoured with the other refereeing team on the night. A Grecians side fighting to stay in League One pushed the Royals on the night and were unfortunate not to end Reading's seven-match unbeaten run, missing two or three big chances in what looked to be a tired performance from the hosts. Turning slightly ill-tempered come full-time, five yellow cards were dished out to the visitors and both squads came together at the end of the game in an angry outburst on the pitch.

arts.ttl: Reading defender earns nomination for award after Birmingham City goal line clearance
arts.sum: Reading defender Amadou Mbengue has been nominated for EFL League One Player of the Month for February. The 23-year-old missed most of January with injury but returned and played in every fixture the Royals had during a successful February. Remaining unbeaten, with four draws and a win, Noel Hunt's side sit four points outside the play-offs heading into Saturday's trip to relegation-threatened Crawley Town. During this time, clean sheets were earned against play-off chasing Huddersfield Town and runaway leaders Birmingham City.

arts.ttl: MP to call for 'Reading test' on powers of Government's football regulator
arts.sum: In a Westminster Hall debate on football sustainability, Yuan Yang will call for more powers for the Government’s Independent Football Regulator, the legislation for which has been stalled by Conservative peers in the House of Lords. Ms. Yang is the member of parliament for Earley and Woodley, which includes Whitley, Shinfield and Sonning, home to Reading Football Club. In the last five years alone, Reading fans have not only seen severe financial mismanagement of their club, but an overwhelming lack of transparency from the owners. The club has slid towards insolvency over the past few years. Staff have been paid late, multiple winding-up petitions have been issued by HMRC, and staff have been forced to endure redundancies and late wage payments on multiple occasions.

arts.ttl: Preview: Crawley Town vs. Reading - prediction, team news, lineups
arts.sum: Sports Mole previews Saturday's League One clash between Crawley Town and Reading, including predictions, team news and possible lineups.
